name: Fort Irwin
tagline: The Army's National Training Center, a remote force-on-force proving ground in the Mojave Desert.
_note: Army National Training Center (Mojave Desert, ~37 mi NE of Barstow; remote). Facts verified from official sources (home.army.mil/irwin, weed-irwin.tricare.mil, DeCA/AAFES, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). BAH MHA CA028 (Barstow/Fort Irwin) via the bah_rates single source of truth. Most families live on post given the remote location. Housing partner not named pending confirmation. California is TRICARE West. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 92311 (Barstow).

data.Location: Mojave Desert, CA (~37 mi NE of Barstow)
data.Setting: Remote high desert; most families live on post

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Fort Irwin is the Army's National Training Center, where brigades rotate through large-scale, force-on-force exercises against a dedicated opposing force, the 11th Armored Cavalry Regiment. It is one of the Army's premier combat-training installations.
It sits deep in the Mojave Desert about 37 miles northeast of Barstow, which shapes everything about living here: it is remote, self-contained, and tight-knit.

sections.title: A Remote Desert Post: Plan to Live On Base

data.subtitle: Fort Irwin is genuinely isolated, so the on-base versus off-base question mostly answers itself: the great majority of families live on post, because the nearest town is a 37-mile drive.
intro: The NTC's location in the open Mojave means there is no commuter town next door. Barstow, about 37 miles away on I-15, is the only real off-post option, so most families with children live on post.

On-base living is convenient and community-focused, with schools, a hospital, and services on the installation. The trade is the remoteness and the desert climate, with the nearest big city (and airport) a longer drive toward Las Vegas or the Inland Empire.

recommendations.advice: Given the 37-mile drive to town, the great majority of families live on post, close to work, schools, and Weed Army Community Hospital.
Barstow is the only practical off-post community; expect the long daily commute if you choose it.
Summers are extreme; plan for heat and budget for the drive when you stock up or travel.
Isolation makes the on-post community close; lean into unit and family programs to make the tour.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Fort Irwin site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Fort Irwin has privatized on-post family housing, and because the post is remote, most families live on post rather than commuting from Barstow.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA CA028 (Barstow/Fort Irwin)

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend public schools, including schools on the installation operated by the Silver Valley Unified School District. Off post, Barstow Unified serves the nearest town.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: A modern hospital on post with primary care, pediatrics, behavioral health, emergency care, pharmacy, and lab

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves. At remote Fort Irwin most families simply take on-post housing, but here's the general framework if you're weighing Barstow.
rent: Your tour is short or your orders feel uncertain.
You prefer on-post housing for the short commute and community.
You'd rather not own in a small, isolated market.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent if you go off post.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years and want to settle in Barstow.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity.
You're comfortable with the long commute and a small resale market.
You've found a home you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but the remoteness leads most Fort Irwin families to live on post. If you consider Barstow, weigh the 37-mile commute and a small market. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics, and at a remote post like this, planning ahead matters even more.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging before you arrive; off-post options are far away. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Most families live on post here; get on the housing list early.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact Silver Valley Unified (on post) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at Weed Army Community Hospital.

data.a: It's the Army's National Training Center, where units rotate through large force-on-force exercises against the 11th Armored Cavalry Regiment, in the Mojave Desert.

data.q: Do families live on or off post?
data.a: Mostly on post. The nearest town, Barstow, is about 37 miles away, so the great majority of families with children live on the installation.
High desert: hot summers, cooler nights, and isolation. Plan for the heat and the drive for big-city amenities.
Fort Irwin is MHA CA028 (Barstow/Fort Irwin). Use the calculator above for your grade.

sections.title: Food & Things Nearby
sections.note: Live nearby places via Google Places. The post is remote, so tap 'Use my location' to re-center on where you are.

picks.name: Barstow
picks.blurb: The nearest city, with shopping, Route 66 history, and outlet malls, about 37 miles away.

picks.name: Calico Ghost Town
picks.blurb: A restored Old West silver-mining town near Barstow.

picks.name: Las Vegas
picks.blurb: The entertainment capital, a couple of hours northeast on I-15.

picks.name: Mojave National Preserve
picks.blurb: Dunes, lava tubes, and Joshua trees across the eastern Mojave.
